title: Dockerfile & Image Builds
description: Learn Dockerfile instructions and docker build options for creating reproducible container images.

tasks.prompt: Build an image tagged 'api:1.0' from the Dockerfile in the current directory.
tasks.explanation: docker build reads the Dockerfile and context path to create an image.

tasks.prompt: Rebuild image 'api:1.0' without using the build cache.
tasks.explanation: --no-cache forces every Dockerfile step to run fresh.

tasks.prompt: Build using Dockerfile 'Dockerfile.prod' and tag the result as 'api:prod'.

tasks.explanation: --build-arg supplies values referenced by ARG instructions.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to use 'node:20-alpine' as the base image.
tasks.explanation: FROM sets the parent image for subsequent Dockerfile layers.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to set working directory to '/app'.
tasks.explanation: WORKDIR creates and sets the default directory for later commands.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to copy all files from build context into '/app'.
tasks.explanation: COPY transfers files from the build context into the image filesystem.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to run 'npm ci' during the image build.
tasks.explanation: RUN executes commands in a temporary container layer during build.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to document that the container listens on port 3000.
tasks.explanation: EXPOSE documents intended container ports for operators and tooling.

tasks.prompt: Write the Dockerfile instruction to run 'npm start' as the default container command.
tasks.explanation: CMD defines the default process when a container starts without overrides.
